Season: Northern Lights (Aurora Borealis) above glacier lagoon in Iceland. This magnificent natural phenomenon has inspired people for centuries and is definitely worth adding to any bucket list.

Description: The Great Sphinx of Giza, one of the most iconic ancient monuments in Egypt and a testament to ancient Egyptian civilization.
The Taj Mahal, an ivory-white marble mausoleum in India and one of the Seven Wonders of the World. The weather here is a bit cloudy and the sun is about to fall.
Arashiyama Bamboo Grove in Kyoto, Japan. A forest made up of soaring bamboo stalks that create almost entirely green tunnels with dappled sunlight.
Vatnajökull National Park in Iceland, featuring Europe's largest glacier with natural ice sculptures, sapphire-colored icebergs, and blue ice caves.
Li River in Guangxi, southern China, offering 85 kilometers of incredible scenery perfect for river cruises through stunning landscapes.
Bagan, Myanmar - an archeological area with more than 2,000 Buddhist temples, pagodas and monuments dating back to the ninth century kingdom of Pagan.
Reine, a fishing village in Norway's Lofoten Islands above the Arctic Circle with a population of about 300. Perfect place to witness the northern lights.

Season: Machu Picchu, Peru - one of the new seven wonders of the world. A dramatic citadel of stone discovered by Hiram Bingham in 1911, built in the center of a sacred landscape.

Description: Waitomo Glowworm Caves on New Zealand's North Island, illuminated by thousands of bioluminescent glowworms creating the illusion of a starry sky overhead.
